name: DemoFoundry — A tour of DemoFoundry
description: A self-running tour of DemoFoundry's own web UI: the Library, the New demo form, choosing a capture mode, and the live script preview. Driven by Playwright against the app on localhost:3000.

steps.narration_text: This is DemoFoundry. It turns a walkthrough of your app into a narrated, captioned demo video. And the demo you're watching right now was made by DemoFoundry, of DemoFoundry — driving its own interface.
Everything you build lives here in the Library: each project, how many scenes it has, and whether it's been rendered yet.
To start a new demo, you click New demo.
First you choose how to capture. A web app can be driven automatically in a browser — that's this path. A desktop app you record yourself. We're demoing a web app, so we'll pick Web app.
You give the demo a name — this is just the label you'll see in your Library.
Then you point it at the running app you want to demo — usually your own app on localhost. Stable test IDs make the capture most reliable.
Now the script. You drop in a steps.json — an ordered list of actions and the words to narrate. We'll load the sample to see what one looks like.
As you type, DemoFoundry reads the script live and shows you exactly what it understood: every scene, the action it'll take, and where it'll zoom or highlight.

From here you'd pick a voice and hit render. DemoFoundry drives your app once, narrates each scene, syncs the video to the audio, and composes a finished MP4 with captions. That's the whole loop — point it at your app, give it a script, and ship a polished demo.
